metadata.title: States PCS: Punjab GK Questions for PPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which instrument is linked to Punjab’s Sikh Dhadhi music tradition?
questions.explanation: Sarangi, along with the dhadh, is central to the Sikh Dhadhi tradition in Punjab, dating to Guru Har Gobind Ji’s era. Dhadhi singers use sarangi to accompany narratives of Sikh history, bravery, and spirituality. The tradition continues as a vital part of Punjab’s musical heritage.

questions.question: Which harvest festival is linked to the origin of Bhangra dance in Punjab?
questions.explanation: Bhangra, Punjab’s iconic folk dance, has origins in the spring harvest festival Baisakhi. It was traditionally performed by farmers to celebrate a good harvest. Other Punjabi dances include Jhumar and Giddha. Baisakhi holds agricultural and cultural significance in Punjab.

questions.question: Which Sikh Guru introduced martial art Gatka after Guru Arjan Dev’s martyrdom?
questions.explanation: Guru Har Gobind Ji, the sixth Sikh Guru, formalized Gatka after Guru Arjan Dev Ji’s martyrdom. Gatka became a symbol of self-defense and martial prowess, emphasizing the martial tradition in Sikhism. It is performed during Sikh festivals and remains a significant part of Punjabi culture.

questions.question: Who is honored at the February Roshni Fair in Jagraon?
questions.explanation: The Roshni Fair in Jagraon, held 23-25 Phagun, honors Muslim saint Pir Abdul Kader Jalani. Devotees from various communities participate, lighting lamps, singing bolian, and dancing, reflecting the syncretic culture of Punjab. Other options refer to local Sikh religious sites and figures.

questions.question: Which Sikh Guru founded both Maghi Mela and Hola Mohalla in Punjab?
questions.explanation: Maghi Mela commemorates the 1705 Battle of Muktsar involving Guru Gobind Singh and forty Mukhte. Hola Mohalla, also initiated by Guru Gobind Singh, takes place in Anandpur Sahib after Holi, highlighting martial skills and Sikh valor. Both festivals are significant events in Punjab.

questions.question: Which Punjab district had the highest literacy rate in Census 2011?
questions.explanation: According to Census 2011, Hoshiarpur recorded Punjab’s highest literacy rate at 84.6%, alongside Pathankot. Punjab’s overall literacy rate in 2011 was 75.84%, with Mansa having the lowest at 61.8%.

questions.question: Which city is home to Punjab Agricultural University?
questions.explanation: Punjab Agricultural University, established in 1962, is located in Ludhiana and played a key role in India’s Green Revolution. It is among India’s oldest agricultural universities, with significant contributions to crop research and extension in Punjab and Northern India. Punjabi University is in Patiala.

questions.question: Which Punjab district has the largest Scheduled Caste population?
questions.explanation: Shaheed Bhagat Singh Nagar has Punjab’s highest Scheduled Caste population, as per Census 2011. The main SC groups in Punjab are Megh, Mazhabi, Chamar, and Balmiki. Constitution Article 46 guarantees social and economic welfare for Scheduled Castes in India.

questions.question: Which district in Punjab has the largest child population?
questions.explanation: Ludhiana leads in Punjab’s child population with 363,086 children, as per recent data. Other populous districts include Amritsar and Jalandhar, but Ludhiana remains the highest. Shaheed Bhagat Singh Nagar records the lowest. Such demographic data is essential for planning state health and education schemes.

questions.question: What was Punjab’s cropping intensity in 2022-23?
questions.explanation: Punjab’s cropping intensity reached 191.7% in 2022-23, indicating extensive multiple cropping. It has increased from about 180% in early 1990s and has remained above 190% since 2015-16. National average is significantly lower, making Punjab’s value among the highest in India.
